name: rockyou.txt
purpose: The legendary list from the RockYou leak (2009) - the most universal wordlist of real human passwords. The default baseline for practically any dictionary attack.
whenToUse: The first wordlist for brute-forcing hashes or online spraying. On a fresh Kali it ships compressed - first unpack it with gunzip /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz.
size: ~14.3 million lines (~134 MB unpacked; ~51 MB as .gz on Kali)

name: rockyou-75.txt (and the -05 ... -75 slices)
purpose: Trimmed slices of rockyou by coverage percentage (rockyou-05.txt ... rockyou-75.txt): smaller size while keeping the most likely passwords. Handy when the full rockyou is too heavy.
whenToUse: For fast online spraying or weak hardware: take rockyou-05/-10/-25 for speed instead of the full list.
size: from a few hundred KB (-05) to a few MB (-75)

name: xato-net-10-million-passwords-1000000.txt
purpose: The top 1 000 000 passwords from the xato "10 million passwords" dump - sorted by frequency. One of the most effective general wordlists for offline cracking.
whenToUse: When rockyou produced no results and you need a large wordlist sorted by probability for cracking hashes.

name: xato-net-10-million-passwords-1000.txt / -100.txt
purpose: The shortest xato slices - the top 1000 and top 100 most common passwords. A minimal set for fast spraying without locking out accounts.
whenToUse: For careful password spraying across a domain (1-2 passwords per account), when it is important not to trigger a lockout.

name: probable-v2_top-12000.txt
purpose: A list of the "most probable" passwords from the probable-wordlists project: the top 12000 candidates that give a high hit rate at a small size.
whenToUse: When you need a compact yet effective wordlist - a balance between speed and coverage when cracking hashes.

name: darkweb2017_top-10000.txt
purpose: The top 10000 passwords collected from darknet dumps (2017). Reflects genuinely leaked, current user passwords.
whenToUse: As a complement to rockyou/xato to cover more "recent" leaked passwords during offline cracking.

name: top-passwords-shortlist.txt
purpose: A very short list of the most common passwords (like Password1, Welcome1, Summer2024). Tuned for careful spraying against corporate accounts.
whenToUse: For the first password-spraying pass in AD, when you need to test a couple of obvious passwords across all users.

name: 100k-most-used-passwords-NCSC.txt
purpose: The top 100000 passwords per the UK NCSC data (based on Have I Been Pwned leaks). A good medium-sized wordlist from an official source.
whenToUse: When you need a reliable "medium" wordlist between the shortlist and the multi-million lists for cracking.

name: weakpass (weakpass.com / zzzteph/weakpass)
purpose: An online source of huge cracking wordlists: aggregated password dumps ranging from kilobytes to hundreds of gigabytes (weakpass_3, weakpass_4, etc.). Not included in Kali - downloaded separately.
whenToUse: When the standard SecLists/rockyou lists were not enough and you need the most complete wordlist for heavy offline GPU cracking.

name: top-usernames-shortlist.txt
purpose: A short list of the most typical account names (admin, root, administrator, user, test, etc.). A baseline set for fast login enumeration.
whenToUse: The first pass when brute-forcing services (SSH, FTP, web forms) when the username is unknown.

name: xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt
purpose: A large wordlist of real usernames from the xato dump - millions of logins sorted by frequency. The primary list for username enumeration/brute-forcing.
whenToUse: When the shortlist did not work and you need to broadly enumerate usernames (for example, kerbrute userenum).

name: names.txt (Usernames/Names)
purpose: A list of common personal names (first names), used to generate logins like jsmith, john.smith when attacking corporate AD accounts.
whenToUse: For building username candidates from corporate naming schemes during AD/email enumeration.

name: default-passwords.csv
purpose: A consolidated CSV table of default credentials for hundreds of devices and products (vendor, login, password). The main reference for default creds in SecLists.
whenToUse: When you discover a network device/service - check the factory login/password before a full brute-force.

name: ftp-betterdefaultpasslist.txt
purpose: A list of default login:password pairs for FTP services, in a format convenient for feeding directly into brute-force tools.
whenToUse: When an open FTP is found - a fast run of factory credentials before a full brute-force.

name: Default-Credentials/Routers/ (Cisco and routers)
purpose: A directory of default credentials by network-equipment vendor: separate files like cisco_default-passwords.txt / cisco_default-users.txt (and hundreds of other vendors).
whenToUse: When working with routers/switches (including Cisco) - to find the factory logins and passwords of a specific vendor.
size: hundreds of small files by vendor (a few KB each)

name: cirt-default (usernames + collection)
purpose: The classic CIRT.net database of default credentials: a list of standard usernames and a consolidated login/password collection covering 500+ vendors.
whenToUse: As an extended reference for default creds, when default-passwords.csv did not cover the needed device.
